subject: [PATCH v6 18/20] nfsd: properly track requested child attributes

2026-06-11.patch_summary: This patch improves the way NFSd tracks and encodes child attributes in delegation requests. It introduces a union of requested and supported child attributes, ensuring that only relevant attributes are sent to clients. This change affects various parts of the NFSd codebase, including nfs4proc.c, nfs4state.c, and nfs4xdr.c. The patch aims to enhance the accuracy and efficiency of attribute encoding in delegation requests.

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that when a client requests any supported child attribute in word 1, it can make gddr_child_attributes[1] non-zero, leading to nfsd4_encode_bitmap4() emitting a two-word bitmap, and nfsd4_get_dir_delegation_rsize() still budgeting only the old one-word child-attribute bitmap, resulting in NFS4ERR_RESOURCE/REP_TOO_BIG errors when encoding fails near the reply/slot limit.
reviews.raw_body:
When a client requests any supported child attribute in word 1, this can
make gddr_child_attributes[1] non-zero, so nfsd4_encode_bitmap4() emits a
two-word bitmap. nfsd4_get_dir_delegation_rsize() still budgets only the
old one-word child-attribute bitmap before executing this non-idempotent
op, so a compound near the reply/slot limit can grant a directory
delegation and then fail encoding with NFS4ERR_RESOURCE/REP_TOO_BIG,
leaving the client without the returned stateid.


-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch improves the handling of child attributes in NFSv4 delegation by tracking the union of requested and supported attributes, and only encoding the attributes in that union when sending add/remove/rename updates. This ensures that the client receives accurate information about the file system's capabilities and limitations. The patch modifies several files to implement this change, including nfsd/nfs4proc.c, nfsd/nfs4state.c, and nfsd/nfs4xdr.c.
